final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk from the Cushman & Wakefield MARKETBEAT BENGALURU OFFICE Q1 2025 report provides an overview of Bengaluru's office market fundamentals and economic indicators for Q1 2025. It details leasing momentum, supply additions, and rental trends, including historical data from 2020 and forecasts for 2025.

Chunk Content: # MARKETBEAT BENGALURU OFFICE Q1 2025

## MARKET FUNDAMENTALS

| Indicator | Value | YOY Chg | Outlook |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Vacancy Rate** | 9.7% | [Up Arrow] | [Up Arrow] |
| **Q1-25 Net Absorption (MSF)** | 2.5 | [Down Arrow] | [Up Arrow] |
| **Stock Wtd. Rent (PSFPM)** | INR 94.0 | [Up Arrow] | [Stable/Dash] |

## ECONOMIC INDICATORS

| Indicator | Value | YOY Chg | Outlook |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **GDP Growth Q3 FY24-25** | 6.20% | [Down Arrow] | [Up Arrow] |
| **CPI Inflation** | 3.60% | [Down Arrow] | [Down Arrow] |
| **Services PMI (3 months average)** | 58.3 | [Up Arrow] | [Stable/Dash] |

**Source:** MOSPI, RBI, HSBC

---

### STRONG LEASING MOMENTUM DRIVEN BY GCCS
Bengaluru recorded gross leasing volumes (GLV) of around 4.9 msf in Q1, continuing the robust leasing activity witnessed over the past several quarters. While quarterly GLV was lower on an annual basis, in comparison to the exceptionally strong quarter in Q1 last year, the city remained the premier destination for global capability centres (GCCs) of multinational corporations. GCC transactions accounted for around 48% of quarterly GLV with multinationals across various sectors taking up large spaces.

Fresh leasing accounted for over 90% of quarterly GLV, highlighting the increasing space requirement with occupiers pursuing expansion and higher numbers of employees returning back to offices. IT-BPM (32%), BFSI (22%) and engineering & manufacturing (17%) were the top 3 sectors during Q1 with flex operators (11%) continuing to expand their footprint. The Outer Ring Road (ORR) submarket contributed over 70% of Q1 GLV, with Peripheral South at a distant second spot (9%). Net absorption stood at 2.5 msf in the quarter, driven by fresh space demand and operationalization of projects with precommitments.

### HEALTHY SUPPLY ADDITION IN Q1
Bengaluru recorded new supply of 3.3 msf in Q1, a growth of 52% on a yoy basis. Outer Ring Road contributed 56% of quarterly supply and Peripheral East (Whitefield) accounted for 30%. The supply pipeline for the upcoming quarters remains strong with several projects awaiting occupancy certificate (OC); nearly 20 msf is estimated to enter the market in 2025. Headline city-wide vacancy rate increased marginally by 30 bps on qoq basis to 9.7%.

### STABLE OFFICE RENTALS ON A QUARTERLY BASIS
Headline office rentals remained stable on a quarterly basis though there was marginal growth as compared to the same period last year. Despite strong space demand, rentals are likely to remain range-bound in the near term due to the expected healthy supply. However, Grade A properties in prime micro markets will continue to command higher rentals.

---

### NET ABSORPTION / NEW SUPPLY
| Year | Net Absorption (msf) | New Supply (msf) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| 2020 | ~7.5 | ~13.0 |
| 2021 | ~8.0 | ~10.5 |
| 2022 | ~11.0 | ~12.5 |
| 2023 | ~14.0 | ~15.5 |
| 2024 | ~16.5 | ~15.0 |
| Q1 2025 | 2.5 | 3.3 |
| 2025F | ~14.0 | ~20.0 |

**Caption:** Net Absorption and New Supply in million square feet (msf) from 2020 to 2025 Forecast.

### OVERALL VACANCY & STOCK WTD. QUOTED RENT
| Year | Stock Wtd. Quoted Rents (INR PSFPM) | Overall Vacancy (%) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| 2020 | ~80 | ~5.0% |
| 2021 | ~82 | ~8.0% |
| 2022 | ~85 | ~10.0% |
| 2023 | ~88 | ~9.0% |
| 2024 | ~92 | ~9.4% |
| Q1 2025 | 94.0 | 9.7% |
| 2025F | ~94 | ~10.0% |

**Caption:** Overall Vacancy (%) and Stock Weighted Quoted Rentals (INR PSFPM) from 2020 to 2025 Forecast.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk, extracted from the Cushman & Wakefield "MARKETBEAT BENGALURU OFFICE Q1 2025" report, provides detailed market statistics for Bangalore's office sector. It covers Q1 2025 performance across various submarkets, including current data and a forecast for planned construction until 2027.

Chunk Content: ## MARKET STATISTICS

| SUBMARKET | INVENTORY (SF) | OVERALL VACANCY RATE | CURRENT QUARTER NET ABSORPTION (SF) | YTD NET ABSORPTION (SF) | YTD LEASING ACTIVITY (SF) | YTD CONSTRUCTION COMPLETIONS (SF) | PLANNED & UNDER CONSTRUCTION (SF)^ | GRADE A STOCK WEIGHTED AVERAGE RENT (INR/SF/M)* |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| CBD/Off CBD | 8,090,906 | 2.5% | 39,763 | 39,763 | 21,200 | - | 681,000 | ₹ 162.1 |
| Outer Ring Road | 90,135,401 | 6.3% | 1,142,184 | 1,142,184 | 3,488,100 | 1,839,716 | 19,012,534 | ₹ 103.7 |
| Peripheral East | 43,206,992 | 15.6% | 814,300 | 814,300 | 306,328 | 1,015,000 | 10,040,115 | ₹ 70.5 |
| Peripheral North | 14,695,898 | 21.7% | 201,562 | 201,562 | 201,678 | 40,608 | 7,738,139 | ₹ 74.6 |
| Peripheral South | 12,982,885 | 11.5% | 129,808 | 129,808 | 422,000 | 180,000 | 2,238,518 | ₹ 68.6 |
| Suburban East | 22,846,292 | 3.0% | 99,200 | 99,200 | 124,254 | - | 1,250,185 | ₹ 135.4 |
| Suburban North West | 2,848,000 | 46.8% | 29,250 | 29,250 | 49,250 | - | 2,561,825 | ₹ 125.0 |
| Suburban South | 9,449,337 | 4.8% | 35,814 | 35,814 | 245,275 | 200,000 | 3,845,607 | ₹ 90.3 |
| **BENGALURU TOTALS** | **204,255,711** | **9.7%** | **2,491,881** | **2,491,881** | **4,858,085** | **3,275,324** | **47,367,923** | **₹ 94.00** |

The report highlights Grade A details only. Certain indicators are historically corrected by addition / deletion of older / refurbished projects as per grade A classification and accounting for changes in built-up / leasable area besides adjusting tenant leases to reflect accurate market conditions.
Net absorption refers to the incremental new space take-up. YTD gross leasing activity includes pre commitments and term renewals.
^Includes planned & under construction projects until 2027. * Stock weighted average asking rental rates for spaces that provide core facility, high-side air conditioning and 100% power back up

**Key to submarkets:**
CBD/Off-CBD – M.G. Road, Millers Road, Vittal Mallya Road, Residency Road, etc.; Peripheral South – Electronic City, Hosur Road, Mysore Road; Outer Ring Road – Sarjapur, KR Puram, Hebbal; Suburban East – Indira Nagar, Old Airport Road, C.V. Raman Nagar; Peripheral East – Whitefield; Suburban South – Koramangala, Bannerghatta Road, Jayanagar; Peripheral North – Bellary Road, Thanisandra Road, Tumkur Road; Suburban Northwest – Rajaji Nagar, Malleshwaram.

US$ 1 = 86.7 INR € 1 = 90.5 INR
Numbers for the first quarter are based on market information collected until 20th March 2025

## KEY LEASE TRANSACTIONS Q1 2025

| PROPERTY | SUBMARKET | TENANT | SF | TYPE |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Bagmane Capital Memphis | Outer Ring Road | American Express | 200,000 | Precommitment |
| Bagmane Constellation Orion | Outer Ring Road | Amazon | 196,000 | Fresh Lease |
| Karle Town Centre – Hub 1 | Outer Ring Road | Publicis | 182,000 | Term Renewal |
| Prestige Tech Park - Venus | Outer Ring Road | Nutanix | 80,225 | Fresh Lease |
| Godrej Centre - Hebbal | Peripheral North | Hitachi Energy | 58,932 | Fresh Lease |

## KEY CONSTRUCTION COMPLETIONS Q1 2025

| PROPERTY | SUBMARKET | MAJOR TENANT | SF | OWNER/DEVELOPER |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Capitaland ITPB MTB-6 | Peripheral East | NA | 845,000 | Capitaland |
| Embassy Tech Village Parcel 8 (Blocks A, B, C, D) | Outer Ring Road | Intuit, JP Morgan | 1,839,716 | Embassy REIT |

---

Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE: CWK) is a leading global commercial real estate services firm for property owners and occupiers with approximately 52,000 employees in nearly 400 offices and 60 countries. In 2023, the firm reported revenue of $9.5 billion across its core services of property, facilities and project management, leasing, capital markets, and valuation and other services. It also receives numerous industry and business accolades for its award-winning culture and commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI), sustainability and more.
